title: Autodiff: Softmax Backward
description:
The forward pass is $f(x) = \text{softmax}(x)$ applied along the last dimension of $x \in \mathbb{R}^{4\times 5}$, i.e. $s_{i,j} = \frac{e^{x_{i,j}}}{\sum_k e^{x_{i,k}}}$.

Given the upstream gradient $g = \frac{\partial L}{\partial f(x)}$ (same shape as the output), implement `softmax_backward(x, g)` returning $\frac{\partial L}{\partial x}$.

For a single row, softmax's Jacobian gives $\frac{\partial L}{\partial x_i} = s_i \left(g_i - \sum_k g_k s_k\right)$. Written per-row this is $dx = s \odot \left(g - (g \cdot s)\right.\text{summed over the last dim, keepdims}\left.\right)$, applied independently to each of the 4 rows.

**Shapes**: `x`: (4, 5), `g`: (4, 5) -> returns `dx`: (4, 5).
starter_code:
import torch

def softmax_backward(x, g):
    # dx given upstream grad g, where forward(x) = torch.softmax(x, dim=-1)
    # TODO
    pass
hints: Recompute s = softmax(x, dim=-1); the softmax Jacobian per row is diag(s) - s s^T, so g^T J = s * (g - sum(g*s)).
Compute the per-row scalar (g*s).sum(-1, keepdim=True) first, subtract it from g, then multiply elementwise by s: dx = s * (g - (g*s).sum(-1, keepdim=True)).

solutions.name: Row-wise softmax Jacobian contraction
solutions.explanation: With $s=\text{softmax}(x)$, the softmax Jacobian per row is $J = \text{diag}(s) - ss^\top$, so $dx = J^\top g = s\odot g - s(s\cdot g) = s\odot\left(g - \sum_k g_k s_k\right)$, computed independently per row via a keepdim sum along the last axis.
solutions.complexity: Time O(n*m), Space O(n*m) for shape (n, m)
